title: Credit Crunch: Bright Meadow’s Rilander on MBS, Housing Policy
summary: “There are a lot of people who would love to give away their prepayment option to get their family into the home they want,” says Adam Rilander, CIO and head of agency MBS strategies at Bright Meadow, in this episode of Credit Crunch. Rilander joins Bloomberg Intelligence’s Erica Adelberg and Noel Hebert to discuss housing affordability, potential unintended consequences of mortgage policy, investment opportunities across the MBS landscape and why higher-coupon paper may be undervalued. title: Yen’s Worst Week Since May Brings It Close to 165 Versus Dollar
summary: The yen is on track for its biggest weekly loss in more than two months, with intervention warnings doing little to deter investors betting on further weakness.

title: Gold Edges Up as Traders Weigh Mideast Conflict, Fed Rate Path
summary: Gold edged higher, holding firmly above the key resistance point of $4,000 an ounce even as the widening conflict in the Middle East fuels expectations for tighter monetary policy.

title: Dangote Targets $100 Billion Empire With Oil and Fertilizers
summary: Aliko Dangote is betting that Africa’s largest refinery, the continent’s biggest cement producer and his fertilizer businesses will expand his empire fivefold to $100 billion by the end of the decade.

title: Stocks Get Some Relief as Brent Slips Below $100: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks are finding some respite after posting their biggest drop this month as Brent crude eased below $100 a barrel and bond yields halted their ascent.

title: Wheat Extends July Rally as War Risks Stoke Fears Over Supply
summary: Wheat climbed to a two-year high in Chicago and Paris, as escalating geopolitical tensions and worsening weather in key producing regions continued to stoke supply concerns.

title: Travelers Draws Wave of Bears as Stock Eyes Best Year Since 2000
summary: The best year for Travelers Companies Inc. shares in more than 25 years has unleashed a bevy of downgrades as Wall Street bets that the stock’s gains have peaked.

title: ‘Ridiculously Tight’ US Preferred Spreads Risk Trapping Buyers
summary: US banks are raising regulatory capital at the tightest valuations since the financial crisis, unsettling the most seasoned investors.

title: Geopolitics and Tech Woes Put Traders on Edge Before Weekend
summary: Even as US stock futures point to tentative gains on Wall Street, AI spending plans and Middle East tensions give plenty to worry about

title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger as India Student Protests Widen
summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh bid to calm mounting anger over examination paper leaks as students continued nationwide protests on Friday, with demands growing for the education minister to step down.

title: Dollar Set for Best Week in a Month as Haven Flows Offer Support
summary: The dollar is heading for its best week in a month as investors return to the traditional haven amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

title: Gulf States Set for Debt Binge to Build Routes Avoiding Hormuz
summary: Traders are preparing for a spike in borrowing from Persian Gulf nations as the region’s oil exporters seek financing to build costly bypasses around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
summary: Brent crude retreated after hitting $100 a barrel in the previous session on concern over widening risks to Middle East supplies, as US President Donald Trump’s revived trade tariffs posed economic headwinds.

title: Global Bonds Are Reeling as Oil Surge Renews Inflation Threat
summary: Global bonds are being pummeled by the latest resurgence in energy prices, delivering losses to investors who bet the worst of this year’s rout was over and teeing up credibility tests for central bankers.

title: Euronext Mulls Setting Up a Private Market to Rival the UK’s
summary: Euronext NV is considering creating a market for private securities, potentially ramping up competition with the London Stock Exchange Group Plc as bourses seek to offer investors access to fast-growing private companies.

title: BofA’s Raedler Says Investors Are Ignoring Stock-Market Risks
summary: Stock markets are pricing in a scenario where everything goes right, leaving investors uncompensated for mounting risks, according to Sebastian Raedler, head of European equity strategy at Bank of America Corp.

title: Goldman, BofA See 2024 Flashback in China Before Politburo Meets
summary: As the calendar turned to July, Chinese retail sales were decelerating fast, the stock market was in the doldrums and investment unexpectedly turned for the worse. Public spending shrank as local governments struggled with financing.

title: Tanker Exits Red Sea Dark as China Ship Heads Toward Strait
summary: A Greek tanker carrying Saudi crude exited the Red Sea with its transponder off, while another Chinese vessel and Russian cargoes headed toward the Bab el-Mandeb strait, after Houthi attacks heightened risks.

title: Trump to Take Up Manila’s Concerns With Xi, Philippines Says
summary: US President Donald Trump assured Philippine leader Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that he would take up Manila’s concerns when he meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Southeast Asian nation said.

title: Japan Is Whipsawing Wall Street’s CLO Machine, Study Shows
summary: The $1.3 trillion US CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street.
